meta.description: The Crude Oracle research library index. SAMPLE entries — replace with real research notes. See docs/DAILY_UPDATE_GUIDE.md.

notes.title: Spare Capacity: The Market's Thinning Safety Margin
notes.summary: Why effective global spare capacity is more concentrated — and more fragile — than headline numbers suggest, and what that means for tail-risk pricing in crude.
sources: OPEC Monthly Oil Market Report (public pages)

notes.title: UKCS Fiscal Risk: Pricing Policy Uncertainty in North Sea Equities
notes.summary: A framework for valuing UK North Sea producers under alternative windfall-tax and licensing scenarios.

notes.title: The Second LNG Wave: Supply Surge vs Asian Demand Elasticity
notes.summary: Mapping the 2026–2029 liquefaction build-out against price-sensitive Asian demand, and the implications for TTF, JKM and LNG equities.

notes.title: Tanker Cycle Notes: Fleet Age, Orderbook and the Rerouting Premium
notes.summary: Why tanker supply fundamentals remain tight even if geopolitical rerouting normalises.

notes.title: Oil 101: Where Demand Really Comes From
notes.summary: A free primer on the structure of global oil demand — transport, petrochemicals, aviation, industry — and why 'peak demand' timing is contested.

notes.title: Reading Inventory Data Like a Professional
notes.summary: How to interpret weekly EIA inventory reports: headline vs adjustment factors, Cushing, SPR, and product stocks.
